articles.title: Rain alert issued for AP and Telangana, besides heat waves - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
The Meteorological Department has issued a significant weather warning for the states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. Forecasts indicate light to moderate rain across the Telugu states over the next three days, specifically on April 7th, 8th, and 9th. The department has warned of thunderstorms and lightning accompanying the rainfall, with isolated lightning strikes possible. Citizens are advised to remain vigilant and stay in safe locations.
Gusty winds, reaching speeds of 30 to 50 kilometres per hour, are also expected. A cyclonic circulation persists over the South Coastal region and the West-Central Bay of Bengal, situated at an altitude of 1.5 kilometres above sea level.
Impact of Surface Trough
A surface trough over Rayalaseema and Telangana continues to influence weather conditions. Located at an altitude of 0.9 kilometres above sea level, this system is expected to bring rainfall to the affected areas. For today, rain is forecast in districts including Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Khammam, Nalgonda, Mahabubabad, Warangal, Siddipet, Rajanna Sircilla, and Suryapet. Light showers are expected to continue for three days, after which temperatures are likely to increase by around 3°C. From April 10th onwards, dry weather conditions are anticipated in Telangana, with a subsequent rise in temperatures.
Rainfall in Andhra Pradesh
Meteorological officials predict light to moderate rain today in North Andhra and Visakhapatnam, with showers also likely in Coastal Andhra and Rayalaseema regions. Yesterday, parts of Eluru district experienced heavy rainfall, which caused hoardings to topple. Despite the rain, heat is expected to persist elsewhere, with daytime temperatures ranging between 35°C and 38°C.
Lightning warnings have been issued across several parts of Andhra Pradesh. The department urged the public to exercise caution, especially during thunderstorms. Yesterday in Markapuram, two people lost their lives and four others were injured after a lightning strike. Officials advise avoiding sheltering under trees or in open fields during rain and recommend staying away from electronic devices during lightning activity.
Cyclones 'Myla' and 'Valu'
Meanwhile, cyclones 'Myla' and 'Valu' have formed near Australia. Their influence is expected to extend to South India, potentially bringing additional rainfall to the region. Despite this, intense heat conditions are set to persist in various areas, with weather patterns remaining variable across the region.
